source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed., 2021) — Chris McChesney, Sean Covey, Jim Huling, Scott Thele, Beverly Walker
_comment: Topic-router for ambiguous-role D4 WIG-Session / weekly-cadence queries. Tests cover EN/JP/zh-TW. Activation criterion = ambiguous on (role, timing) per description.

test_cases.prompt: Help me with my WIG Session. Not sure how to approach it.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-cadence. 'My WIG Session' is role-ambiguous — could be solo run, member-prep, member-debrief, or leader-facilitate. Router asks the (role, timing) Socratic question and routes to one of 4 atomic skills.
test_cases.notes: positive: matches EN signal 'Help with my WIG Session' literally cited in description

test_cases.prompt: weekly cadence の運営について相談したい。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-cadence. JP weekly-cadence query without role/timing marker. Router disambiguates.
test_cases.notes: positive: matches JP signal 「weekly cadence の運営」 in description

test_cases.prompt: WIG Session 怎麼跑？我們團隊還沒有節奏。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-cadence. zh-TW query — '我們團隊' hints team but speaker role (leader vs member) and timing (before/during/after) still unspecified. Router asks the (role, timing) question.
test_cases.notes: positive: matches zh-TW signal 「WIG Session 怎麼跑」「每週節奏」 in description; 'team' alone doesn't pin facilitator-vs-member

test_cases.prompt: Set up the cadence — what does a good weekly review look like?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-cadence. Generic cadence-setup query, no role marker. Router disambiguates whether user is solo, leader, or member.
test_cases.notes: positive: 'Set up the cadence' listed in description as EN trigger phrasing

test_cases.prompt: I'm running my own solo WIG Session tomorrow morning — walk me through the agenda.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d4-cadence. Role explicit (solo, just me) + timing explicit (during, with prep). Fire 4dx-d4-personal-wig-session directly.
test_cases.notes: lure: explicit solo+during — description excludes role-explicit cases

test_cases.prompt: 我們團隊還沒有 WIG，要怎麼開始 4DX？
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d4-cadence. Topic is wrong — pre-D4. Description explicitly excludes 'before D1-D3 are set'. Route to 4dx-d1-wig-formulation (topic-router) or D1 atomic.
test_cases.notes: lure: wrong topic — surface mentions weekly cadence prerequisite but actual need is D1

test_cases.prompt: I missed my commitment three weeks in a row and I've stopped showing up. Help.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d4-cadence. Cadence has already broken (multiple skipped weeks). Description excludes broken-cadence; route to 4dx-sustain-personal-momentum-rescue first.
test_cases.notes: lure: broken-cadence — description explicitly hands this off to sustain-rescue, not the D4 router

test_cases.prompt: 今度の WIG Session で何を commit するか考えたい。チームで動いてる。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d4-cadence (borderline). Role is implied member (チームで動いてる + commit を考える before session) and timing is before. Strong signal could go directly to 4dx-d4-member-commitment-prep, but the user hasn't fully named role; router's disambiguation Q is still helpful — and acceptable to skip step 2 if the inference is high-confidence per description's 'If signal strong → skip to step 3'.
test_cases.notes: boundary: signal strength borderline. Either router fires (asks confirmation) OR atomic member-commitment-prep fires directly — both acceptable per description's 'detect implicit role' clause

self_evaluation_note: All 8 cases reviewed against description-only activation. 4 should_trigger fire on (role, timing)-ambiguous queries; 3 should_not_trigger lures (explicit solo / pre-D4 / broken-cadence) correctly excluded. Edge case is genuinely borderline because description authorizes 'skip step 2 if signal strong' — pass_rate marked 0.875 to flag this honest tension; if user defines borderline-fires-router as a pass, raise to 1.0.
